Tuberculosis Surveillance

Epidemiological Description of TB Notifications · NMC Surveillance, South Africa

Published

March 13, 2026

Priority condition — Tuberculosis remains a leading cause of morbidity and mortality in South Africa. This report describes the epidemiology of notifiable TB forms based on NMC surveillance data, 2020–present, including drug-resistant TB (MDR-TB and XDR-TB).

NoteReport metadata
Conditions Tuberculosis:pulmonary; Tuberculosis:extra-pulmonary; Tuberculosis: multidrug- resistant (MDR -TB); Tuberculosis: extensively drug -resistant (XDR -TB)
Years 2020 – 2026
Data source NMC Notifiable Medical Conditions surveillance database
Generated 13 March 2026
Note This report covers notifiable TB forms only (pulmonary, extra-pulmonary, MDR-TB, XDR-TB). Routine TB case data is managed separately through the ETR.Net system.

NoteInterpretation Notes

Drug-resistant TB (DR-TB):

  • MDR-TB = Multidrug-resistant TB, resistant to at least isoniazid and rifampicin
  • XDR-TB = Extensively drug-resistant TB, MDR-TB plus resistance to fluoroquinolones and at least one injectable second-line drug

Data limitations:

  • This report covers notifiable TB forms reported through NMC only
  • Routine TB case data is managed through the ETR.Net electronic TB register system
  • Case fatality reflects deaths among notified cases; actual mortality may differ

See Data Quality for reporting completeness and Methods for full definitions.

Report generated: 13 March 2026 | NMC Surveillance Unit
